document.title: eco Specification v1
document.purpose: Describe eco: a filesystem-native execution runner that crystallizes executable directory trees into timestamped run directories and executes them there.
author: Lion Kimbro
Wing-Cat (Chat-GPT)

overview: eco is a generic execution runner.
eco does not know about mills, workflows, pipelines, graphs, or AI systems.
- deep-copy the ecology into that run directory
- execute the ecology from within that copied directory
design-goals: Executable environments should be inspectable after execution.
Execution should occur entirely inside the copied run directory.
The source ecology should never be mutated during execution.
The system should remain small, understandable, and hackable.
relationship-to-lionscliapp: eco is implemented as a lionscliapp application.
eco uses lionscliapp persistent configuration storage.
eco stores persistent configuration in a .eco directory.

keys.execpath.rundir: Parent directory where timestamped run directories are created.
keys.times: Number of runs to perform in series; defaults to 1 and must be a positive integer.

ecology-directory.description: A source directory tree that eco can crystallize into a runnable execution directory.
notes: eco treats the ecology as opaque.
eco does not interpret the contents beyond ecology.json.
The ecology may contain Python programs, shell scripts, assets, configuration files, or arbitrary data.

ecology-json.title: ecology.json
ecology-json.description: Defines how eco executes an ecology.

cwd.description: Working directory relative to the copied run directory.

command.description: Command list supplied directly to subprocess.run().

description: Run directories are allocated under the configured rundir.
Directory names follow the format yyyy-mm-dd_run-####.
Run numbering resets each new day.
The first run number of a day is run-0000.

steps: Load ecology.json from the source ecology directory.
Allocate a timestamped run directory under the configured rundir.
Deep-copy the entire source ecology directory into the allocated run directory.
Execute the declared command using subprocess.run().
Rewrite ECO-RUN.json with final status COMPLETE or FAILED.
If times is greater than 1, repeat the lifecycle in series until the requested count is complete or a run fails.

eco-run-json.title: ECO-RUN.json
eco-run-json.description: Execution metadata written into the run directory by eco.

notes: eco v1 intentionally avoids orchestration complexity.
eco v1 does not define workflow semantics.
eco v1 does not define graph execution.
eco v1 does not define distributed execution.
eco v1 focuses only on reproducible execution crystallization.
The copied run directory is the execution environment.
Execution always occurs inside the copied run directory, never inside the source ecology.
